rules.title: スコアリングエンジン
rules.subtitle: 優先度の重み付けを調整し、カスタムルールを設定します。
rules.diskThresholdsDesc: 各ディスクでクリーンアップが開始（しきい値）および停止（目標）するタイミングを設定します。
rules.cleanupThreshold: クリーンアップしきい値 %
rules.cleanupThresholdDesc: 使用率がこの%を超えたらクリーンアップを開始
rules.cleanupTargetDesc: 使用率がこの%に下がったらクリーンアップを停止
rules.preferenceWeightsDesc: 重みが高いほど、属性の削除スコアへの影響が大きくなります。
rules.customRulesDesc: ルールは次回のエンジン実行時に有効になり、即座には適用されません。複数のルールがアイテムに一致する場合、それらの効果は乗算されます。「常に保持」は絶対的なオーバーライドであり、他のルールでは上書きできません。
rules.noRules: ルールが設定されていません。メディアは優先度の重み付けのみで順位付けされます。
rules.deletionPriorityDesc: 削除優先度順にアイテムがランク付けされています。目標ディスクしきい値に達するのに十分な数のアイテムのみが削除されます。
rules.diskBelowThreshold: ディスク使用量がしきい値以下です。アイテムは削除されません。
rules.noItemsToEvaluate: 評価するアイテムがありません。連携を接続し、メディアが存在することを確認してください。
rules.orderDisclaimer: ルールの順序は整理目的のみです — すべてのルールは位置に関係なく評価されます。

settings.languageDesc: 表示言語を選択してください
settings.engineBehaviorDesc: スコアリングエンジンの結果に対する動作を制御します
settings.scoreTiebreakerDesc: アイテムのスコアが同じ場合、どのように順序付けしますか？
settings.tiebreakerSizeDesc: 最も大きいものから（より多くのスペースを解放）
settings.tiebreakerNameAsc: アルファベット順（A → Z）
settings.tiebreakerOldest: ライブラリ内で最も古いものから
settings.tiebreakerNewest: ライブラリ内で最も新しいものから
settings.noIntegrations: 連携が設定されていません
settings.noIntegrationsHelp: Plex、Sonarr、Radarr、または Tautulli インスタンスに接続して開始します。
settings.changeUsernameDesc: セキュリティ向上のため管理者ユーザー名を更新
settings.changePasswordDesc: 管理者パスワードを更新
settings.confirmPassword: 新しいパスワードを確認
settings.noApiKey: API Key がまだ生成されていません。
settings.apiKeyHashedHint: API key exists but cannot be fully displayed (stored as a hash). Regenerate to see the full key.
settings.deletionSafetyDesc: Capacitarr がメディアファイルを完全に削除できるかどうかを制御します
settings.deletionSafetyExplain: 有効にすると、スコアリングエンジンがアイテムを削除対象としてフラグ付けした場合、Capacitarr はストレージからメディアファイルを完全に削除します。ファイルは復元できません。
settings.enableDeletions: 実際の削除を有効にする
settings.disableDeletions: 実際の削除を無効にする
settings.deletionsActive: フラグ付きアイテムは完全に削除されます
settings.deletionsSimulated: すべての削除はシミュレーションです（ドライラン）
settings.deletionsActiveAlertDesc: スコアリングエンジンによってフラグ付けされたメディアはディスクから完全に削除されます。この設定を無効にしてシミュレーションモードに戻ります。
settings.pollIntervalDesc: Capacitarr が連携をチェックする頻度
settings.pollIntervalHint: ポーラーは動的に調整されます — 再起動は不要です。
settings.logLevelDesc: Controls the verbosity of application logs visible in Docker container output.
settings.logLevelHint: Changes take effect immediately — no restart required.
settings.dataManagementDesc: 監査ログの保持期間を設定
settings.retentionWarning: 無期限の保持はデータベースが継続的に増大する原因となります。最終的にパフォーマンスに影響する可能性があります。
settings.clearAllData: 収集済みデータをすべてクリア
settings.clearDataDesc: Removes all audit logs, capacity history, and engine stats. Disk group thresholds and targets are preserved. Integrations, rules, and preferences are preserved.
settings.typeDeleteToConfirm: 確認のため DELETE と入力してください
settings.checkForUpdatesDesc: Periodically check for new Capacitarr releases. No data is sent — only a public API is queried.
engine.enableAutoMode: 自動モードを有効にしますか？
engine.autoModeWarning: 自動モードはしきい値を超えたメディアアイテムを自動的に削除します。この操作は元に戻せません。よろしいですか？
engine.enableAutoModeConfirm: 自動モードを有効にする
help.title: ヘルプ
help.subtitle: Capacitarr の仕組みと効果的な設定方法を学びましょう。
help.whatsNew.notificationsDesc: Get alerts via Discord, Apprise, or in-app when the engine runs, deletes media, or encounters errors. Configure channels in Settings → Notifications.
help.whatsNew.i18nDesc: Capacitarr is now available in 22 languages. Switch your preferred language from the globe icon in the navigation bar or in Settings → Display.
help.whatsNew.plexOAuthDesc: When adding a Plex integration, you can sign in with your Plex account directly — no need to manually find your token.
help.whatsNew.cascadingRulesDesc: Rules now use a six-level keep/remove spectrum instead of the old protect/target system — with optional service scoping so rules can apply to a specific integration.
help.howScoringWorks: スコアリングの仕組み
help.howScoringWorks.intro: Capacitarr scores each media item from 0 to 1 based on weighted factors. Higher scores mean the item is a better candidate for removal.
help.howScoringWorks.behavior: When disk usage exceeds your threshold, items are evaluated and the highest-scoring ones are removed first — freeing space efficiently while preserving the content you care about most.
help.understandingSliders.intro: Each factor has a weight from 0–10. Higher weight means more influence on the final score. The six factors are:
help.factor.watchHistoryDesc: Has anyone watched this? Unwatched content scores higher (more likely to be removed).
help.factor.lastWatchedDesc: How recently was it watched? Content watched long ago scores higher.
help.factor.fileSizeDesc: Larger files score higher, freeing more space per deletion.
help.factor.seriesStatusDesc: For TV shows: ended series score higher than continuing ones. Movies and other types use a neutral default.
help.readingScoreDetail.intro: Click any row in the Audit History to open its Score Detail modal. It breaks down exactly how the final score was calculated using three columns: Raw, Weight, and Contribution.
help.readingScoreDetail.rawDesc: Represents how strongly this factor suggests the media should be cleaned up. 1.0 = maximum cleanup signal; 0.0 = minimum cleanup signal.
help.rawScore.watchHistoryDesc: 1.0 if never watched, decays with more plays (e.g. 1 play → 0.5, 2 plays → 0.25)
help.rawScore.lastWatchedDesc: 1.0 if watched over a year ago or never, lower if watched recently
help.rawScore.fileSizeDesc: Scales linearly up to 50 GB (1.0), capped at 1.0 for anything larger
help.rawScore.ratingDesc: 1.0 for lowest-rated content (rating 0/10 → raw 1.0, rating 10/10 → raw 0.0)
help.rawScore.timeInLibraryDesc: 1.0 for content in the library for a year or more, scales linearly
help.rawScore.seriesStatusDesc: For shows: 1.0 if ended, 0.2 if continuing. For movies: 0.5 (neutral default)
help.readingScoreDetail.weightDesc: Set by you on the Scoring Engine page. Higher weight = more influence. Each factor's contribution = (rawScore × weight) / totalWeightSum.
help.readingScoreDetail.weightExample: Example: If Watch History has weight 7 and raw score 1.0, and total weights sum to 30, its contribution = 7 / 30 = 0.23.
help.readingScoreDetail.contributionDesc: The actual portion of the final score this factor is responsible for. All contributions sum to the total score. These are shown as the colored segments in the stacked bar at the top of the modal.
help.thresholdAndTarget.desc: The threshold is the disk usage percentage that triggers cleanup. The target is where cleanup stops.
help.thresholdAndTarget.example: Example: threshold 85%, target 75% means cleanup starts at 85% full and continues removing items until usage drops to 75%.
help.customRules.intro: Custom rules let you influence which content gets kept or removed beyond the weighted scoring factors. Each rule matches media by a field (title, genre, tag, rating, etc.) and applies an effect that adjusts the item's deletion score.
help.effect.alwaysKeepDesc: Absolute override — the item is immune to deletion regardless of its score or any other rules.
help.effect.preferKeepDesc: Strongly reduces the deletion score (multiplies by 0.2). The item is unlikely to be removed.
help.effect.leanKeepDesc: Moderately reduces the deletion score (multiplies by 0.5).
help.effect.leanRemoveDesc: Slightly increases the deletion score (multiplies by 1.2).
help.effect.preferRemoveDesc: Significantly increases the deletion score (multiplies by 2.0).
help.effect.alwaysRemoveDesc: Pushes the score to the maximum — the item will be removed first whenever cleanup runs.
help.customRules.conflictDesc: When multiple rules match the same item, their effects multiply together. However, "Always keep" always wins — if any matching rule uses "Always keep", the item is immune to deletion no matter what other rules say.
help.customRules.scopingDesc: Rules can optionally be scoped to a specific integration (e.g., only your Sonarr instance), so different services can have different rule sets.
help.tiebreaker.intro: When two or more items have the same score, the tiebreaker determines which is removed first. Configure this in Settings → Engine Behavior.
help.tiebreaker.largestFirstDesc: Prefer deleting bigger files to free more space (default)
help.tiebreaker.alphabeticalDesc: Sort tied items A → Z by title
help.tiebreaker.oldestFirstDesc: Items added to the library longest ago are removed first
help.tiebreaker.newestFirstDesc: Most recently added items are removed first
help.auditLog.intro: The audit log shows every item the engine evaluated. Each entry shows the score breakdown — hover over the colored bar to see individual factor contributions.
help.auditLog.dryRunDesc: Simulated only; no files were deleted
help.auditLog.queuedDesc: Flagged for manual review before deletion
help.executionModes.dryRunDesc: No files are deleted; the engine only logs what it would do. Safe for testing and tuning your weights.
help.executionModes.approvalDesc: Items are flagged for manual approval before deletion. You review and confirm each removal.
help.executionModes.autoDesc: Items are automatically deleted when thresholds are breached. Use with caution.
help.executionModes.sunsetDesc: Items enter a countdown queue before deletion. A configurable label is applied to items in your media server, and optional poster overlays show "Leaving in X days". If disk usage spikes, items are escalated for immediate removal.
help.executionModes.safetyGuardDesc: Even in Auto or Approval mode, actual file deletion only happens when the "Enable Actual Deletions" toggle is turned on in Settings → Advanced. When disabled, all deletions are simulated (Dry-Delete) regardless of execution mode.
help.faq.engineFrequencyQ: How often does the engine run?
help.faq.engineFrequencyA: The engine runs on the poll interval you configure in Settings (default: every 5 minutes). It checks disk usage, syncs media from your integrations, scores everything, and acts based on your execution mode. You can also trigger a manual run from the Dashboard.
help.faq.integrationsQ: How do I set up integrations?
help.faq.integrationsA: Go to Settings → Integrations and click "Add Integration". Capacitarr supports Sonarr, Radarr, Plex, and Tautulli. Enter your server URL and API key (or use Plex OAuth sign-in for Plex). After adding, the engine syncs on the next poll cycle.
help.faq.deleteHappensQ: What actually happens when something is deleted?
help.faq.deleteHappensA: In Auto or Approval mode (with deletions enabled), Capacitarr tells Sonarr or Radarr to remove the media. The *arr app handles the actual file deletion from disk. Capacitarr itself never touches your files directly.
help.faq.notificationsA: Capacitarr supports Discord webhooks, Apprise, and in-app notifications. Configure them in Settings → Notifications. You can choose which events trigger alerts (engine runs, deletions, errors). Apprise lets you route notifications to dozens of services (email, Telegram, Slack, etc.) through a single Apprise server.
help.faq.languageQ: How do I change the language?
help.faq.languageA: Click the globe icon in the navigation bar to switch languages, or go to Settings → Display. Capacitarr supports 22 languages. The setting is saved in your browser.
help.faq.safeToTestQ: Is it safe to test without deleting anything?
help.faq.safeToTestA: Yes. New installations default to Dry-Run mode and the deletion safety guard is a separate toggle. In Dry-Run mode, the engine evaluates and scores everything but never deletes. You can review results in the Audit Log before switching to a live mode.
help.tipsAndBestPractices: ヒントとベストプラクティス

update.available: A new version of Capacitarr is available.
update.upToDateDesc: You're running the latest version of Capacitarr.

library.noItemsDesc: No media items were returned. Make sure you have integrations configured and enabled in Settings.
library.deleteDesc: These items will be queued for deletion:
library.protectedTooltip: Protected by an always_keep rule — cannot be deleted
library.loadingHint: Large libraries may take a few moments to load
audit.movedToLibrary: The audit log has moved to Library → History

sunset.subtitle: Items scheduled for deletion after a countdown period
deletion.emptyInSunset: Items will appear here when sunset countdowns expire or escalation triggers.
settings.sunsetSettingsDesc: Configure poster overlays and countdown behavior for sunset mode.
settings.posterOverlayDesc: Apply "Leaving in X days" countdown banners to media posters in your media server.
settings.restoreAllPostersDesc: Emergency button: restore all original posters for items currently in the sunset queue.
